theorems.name: Corollary 3.13
theorems.body: There exists a universal constant $c\in (0,\infty )$ such that for every two integers $d,n\ge 3$ we have $$ \mathcal {G}_{n,d}\left (\left \{H\in \mathbb G _n:\ \gamma \!\left (H,d_{\mathscr X _K}^2\right )\ge c(\log _d n)^2\right \}\right )\ge 1-\frac {C(d)}{\sqrt [3]{n}}, $$ where $K,C(d)$ are the constant from Lemma~\ref {lem:random graph in good family} and $\mathscr X _K$ is as in~\eqref {eq:def X_K}.
theorems.slogan: There exists a universal constant such that for all integers d and n at least 3, the probability that a random d-regular graph on n vertices has a certain spectral gap property is at least one minus a term decaying with n. The spectral gap is bounded below by a constant multiple of the square of the logarithm of n base d. The probability bound involves constants from a referenced lemma and a defined set. The result holds with high probability as n grows.

paper.title: Expanders with respect to Hadamard spaces and random graphs

theorems.name: Corollary 2 (Spectral Gap of $ \mathrm{HL}'_2(G) $ for Random Regular Graphs)
theorems.body: Let $ G $ be a random $d$-regular graph on $n$ vertices, and let $ H = \mathrm{HL}'_2(G) $. Then with high probability as $ n \to \infty $, the spectral gap of $ H $ satisfies:\[  \mathrm{Gap}(H) := \lambda _1(H) - \lambda _2(H) \ge d - 2\sqrt{d-1} - o(1),  \]where $ \lambda _1(H) = 2d - 2 $ is the largest eigenvalue of $ H $.
theorems.slogan: The spectral gap of the graph H, derived from a random d-regular graph G, is at least d minus two times the square root of d minus one, minus a negligible term. This holds with high probability as the number of vertices grows large. The largest eigenvalue of H is 2d minus 2. The result provides a lower bound on the difference between the first and second eigenvalues.

theorems.name: Theorem 18 (Friedman~ \cite{friedman03})
theorems.body: For arbitrary $\delta > 0$, a random $d$-regular graph $G$ has probability $1 - o(1)$ (the $o(1)$ term tends to~ 0 as $n$ grows) of satisfying $\lambda (G) \leq 2\sqrt{d-1}+\delta $.
theorems.slogan: A random d-regular graph almost certainly has its second largest eigenvalue bounded by twice the square root of d minus one plus an arbitrarily small positive constant. This holds with probability approaching one as the number of vertices increases. The bound applies to the spectral gap of the graph. The result is asymptotic and holds for any fixed positive delta.

theorems.name: Proposition 5.14
theorems.body: \cite [Theorem 3.1(b)]{Moh88}\cite [Theorem 5.5(b)]{MW89} We have the following inequalities. $\frac {d-d\lambda _0(\Gamma )}{d-2+\lambda _0(\Gamma )}\le i(\Gamma ) \le \sqrt {1 - \lambda _0(\Gamma ))^2}.$
theorems.slogan: The edge isoperimetric constant is bounded below by a function involving the spectral gap and the maximum degree. It is also bounded above by the square root of one minus the spectral gap squared. These inequalities relate expansion properties to spectral data. The result holds for regular graphs.

paper.title: Hausdorff Dimension of non-conical and Myrberg limit sets

theorems.name: Lemma
theorems.body: Suppose graph $G$ has spectral gap $\alpha$ and irregularity $\beta$. Then $ {Reg}(G)$ has spectral gap at least $\beta^{-2}\alpha$.
theorems.slogan: The spectral gap of a graph's regularization is bounded below by the square of the reciprocal of its irregularity multiplied by its original spectral gap.

paper.title: Gossip vs. Markov Chains, and Randomness-Efficient Rumor Spreading

theorems.name: Theorem 4.3
theorems.body: Let $A$ be the adjacency matrix of a random $(d,k)$-regular graph sampled uniformly from $ \mathcal H(n,d,k)$ with $d\geq k\geq 3$, then any eigenvalue $\lambda (A)\not=d(k-1)$ satisfies\[ |\lambda (A)- k+2| \leq 2\sqrt{(k-1)(d-1)}+\epsilon _n, \]asymptotically almost surely with $\epsilon _n\to 0$ as $n\to \infty $.
theorems.slogan: For a random regular graph with given parameters, any eigenvalue not equal to the largest one is concentrated near a specific value. The deviation from this value is bounded by an expression involving the square root of the product of two terms. This bound holds with high probability as the graph size grows. The error term in the bound approaches zero as the number of vertices increases.

theorems.name: Lemma 5
theorems.body: Given $d\ge 3$ and $G\in \mathcal{G}_{\ge d}$, if $\mathrm{gap}(G)\ge \frac{\sqrt{d-1}}{d}$, then $\mathrm{gap}(G)=\frac{\sqrt{d-1}}{d}$ and $G$ is $d$-regular.
theorems.slogan: For graphs with minimum degree at least d, if the spectral gap is at least the square root of d minus one divided by d, then the spectral gap equals that value and the graph is d-regular.

paper.title: Finite projective planes meet spectral gaps

theorems.name: Theorem 4.1
theorems.slogan: For a simple graph with maximum vertex degree Delta, the spectral gap from one for the non-backtracking Laplacian is at least one divided by Delta minus one. This bound is tight. The proof uses matrix norm inequalities and properties of the adjacency matrix. Equality holds for Delta-regular graphs.

paper.title: Spectral theory of the non-backtracking Laplacian for graphs

theorems.name: Lemma 5.3 (\cite{friedman2008proof,bordenave2020new})
theorems.body: For any constant $\Delta \geq 3$, with probability $1 - o_n(1)$ over the choice of a random $\Delta $-regular graph $G = (V,E)$ on $n$ vertices, we have that\[  \left\vert \lambda _{\min }(A_G)\right\vert  \leq (2 + o_n(1))\sqrt{\Delta -1}.  \]
theorems.slogan: For any constant degree at least three, a random regular graph on n vertices almost certainly has its smallest eigenvalue magnitude bounded by approximately twice the square root of the degree minus one. The bound holds with probability approaching one as n grows large. The result applies to the adjacency matrix of the graph.

paper.title: Rapid Mixing on Random Regular Graphs beyond Uniqueness

theorems.name: Theorem 5
theorems.body: Let $Y = Y(n,p;d)$ for $d \geq 2$ and $p = \frac{(1+\varepsilon )d \log n}{n}$, where $\varepsilon >0$. There exists a constant $C > 0$ such that w.h.p. the spectral gap of $Y$ satisfies\begin{equation}   \delta (Y) - C \sqrt{\log n} \leq \lambda (Y) \leq \delta (Y). \end{equation}
theorems.slogan: For a random graph model with specific parameters, the spectral gap is bounded with high probability. The lower bound is given by the minimum degree minus a constant times the square root of the logarithm of n. The upper bound is exactly the minimum degree. These bounds hold when the edge probability is above a certain threshold.

paper.title: Algebraic and combinatorial expansion in random simplicial complexes
